plan.what: Build the complete v0.1.0 of stepgate itself: the package scaffold, the proposal state machine, concurrent session storage, the full command set (init, propose, show, approve, reject, exec-log, verify, close, abandon, next, status, history, doctor), and the automated test suite.
plan.why: This is the first implementation session of the project, working from the finished planning document. Everything else (publishing to TestPyPI, adoption in other projects) depends on a working, tested CLI existing first, so the whole v0.1.0 scope lands as one initial micro-change before the tool can gate its own subsequent development.
plan.how: Python 3.10+ package with a src/ layout and hatchling backend, argparse for the CLI, rich for prose rendering, filelock guarding only stepgate's own state writes under .stepgate/. The state machine is validated in the Proposal model so no command can skip a step, and every event is appended to history.jsonl, which is never rewritten.
plan.expected_result: A locally installed stepgate command whose full protocol cycle works end to end in this very repository, honoring the core principle that it never blocks code, commits, or the user.
plan.verification: pytest suite covering the state machine (including abandon), init idempotency and content preservation, corrupted-state handling without tracebacks, and the narrated end-to-end scenario from the planning doc; plus this real dogfooding cycle recorded in the repo's own .stepgate/.

data.summary: Implemented the full v0.1.0: scaffold, state machine, session store, all 13 commands, rich rendering, and 33 automated tests

data.evidence: pytest: 33 passed; full protocol cycle exercised end-to-end in this repository via the CLI itself

data.suggestion: Build the distribution artifacts (python -m build) and publish to TestPyPI, then PyPI, using an API token
